---
title: "corona virus beliefs analysis"
output:
  html_document: default
  word_document: default
date: "2026-03-12"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)

library(dplyr)
library(readr)

d_coronavirus <- read_csv('../data/life-under-lockdown-wave2-extract.numbers')
```

## Hypothesis

H1 Gender is associated with number of conspiracy beliefs about coronavirus

## Findings

```{r}
d_coronavirus %>%
  group_by(
    gender
  ) %>%
  summarize(
    n = n()
    Mdn = median(beliefs)
    M = mean(beliefs)
    SD = sd(beliefs)
  )
```

```{r}
d_coronavirus %>%
  filter(gender != 'In another way') %>%
  t.test(
    beliefs ~ gender,
    data = .
  )
```

Males in the sample believed in slightly more conspiracy theories than females in the same, but this was not statistically significant, t(494.31) = -1.201, p = .230.
